results.question: Show all students
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM students;
results.system_sql: SELECT * FROM students;

results.question: List all courses
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM courses;
results.system_sql: SELECT * FROM courses;

results.question: Top scoring student
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT student_name, SUM(score) as total_score
FROM students
GROUP BY student_name
ORDER BY total_score DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, MAX(sc.marks) AS top_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id;

results.question: Show student name with course and marks
results.baseline_sql: SELECT student_name, course, marks FROM students;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students and their courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT students.name, courses.name AS course_name
FROM students
INNER JOIN enrollments ON students.id = enrollments.student_id
INNER JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.id;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name AS student_name, c.title AS course_title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: Average marks per student
results.baseline_sql: SELECT AVG(marks) FROM students;

results.question: Top 3 students by marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT student_name, SUM(marks) as total_marks
FROM students
GROUP BY student_name
ORDER BY total_marks DESC
LIMIT 3;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, s.id, ssc.marks
FROM scores ssc
JOIN students s ON ssc.student_id = s.id
ORDER BY ssc.marks DESC
LIMIT 3;

results.question: student_name instead of name
results.baseline_sql: SELECT student_name FROM students;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.id, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: student_scores instead of scores
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM student_scores;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, s.age, s.department, s.id AS student_id, c.id AS course_id, s.id AS student_scores
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: Show best student
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department, c.title, sc.marks, s.exam_date
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
ORDER BY sc.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Top performer
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT name, SUM(score) as total_score
FROM table_name
GROUP BY name
ORDER BY total_score DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, c.title, s.age, s.department, ssc.marks, ssc.exam_date
FROM students s
JOIN scores ssc ON s.id = ssc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON ssc.course_id = c.id
ORDER BY ssc.marks DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: High scoring students
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT student_name, SUM(score) as total_score
FROM scores
GROUP BY student_name
ORDER BY total_score DESC
LIMIT 10;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, c.title
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks >= 80;

results.question: List student emails with course names and marks above average
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.email, c.name, s.mark
FROM Student s
JOIN Course c ON s.course_id = c.id
WHERE c.name IN (
    SELECT name 
    FROM Course 
    GROUP BY name 
    HAVING AVG(mark) > (SELECT AVG(mark) FROM Student)
)
ORDER BY s.email;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.email, c.title, s.marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(s2.marks)
    FROM scores s2
    JOIN students s3 ON s2.student_id = s3.id
    JOIN courses c4 ON s2.course_id = c4.id
    WHERE s3.id = s.id
);

results.question: Students who performed better than average in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT s.student_id, s.name, c.course_name, s.grade
FROM students s
JOIN courses c ON s.course_id = c.course_id
WHERE s.grade > (
    SELECT AVG(s2.grade)
    FROM students s2
    WHERE s.student_id = s2.student_id
);
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title 
FROM students s 
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id 
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id 
WHERE sc.marks > (SELECT AVG(sc2.marks) FROM scores sc2 WHERE sc.course_id = sc2.course_id);

results.question: Find students who scored above average in at least 2 courses
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT student_name, COUNT(*) as num_courses
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.student_id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.course_id
WHERE students.student_id IN (
    SELECT student_id
    FROM enrollments
    GROUP BY student_id
    HAVING AVG(courses.grade) > 3.5
)
GROUP BY students.student_name
HAVING num_courses >= 2;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, s.email, s.age, s.department
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
WHERE sc.marks > (
    SELECT AVG(sc2.marks)
    FROM scores sc2
    JOIN courses c2 ON sc2.course_id = c2.id
    WHERE sc2.student_id = s.id
);

results.question: Top department based on average marks
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT department, AVG(marks) as avg_marks
FROM students
GROUP BY department
ORDER BY avg_marks DESC
LIMIT 1;
results.system_sql:
SELECT department, AVG(marks) AS avg_marks
FROM scores
JOIN students ON scores.student_id = students.id
JOIN courses ON scores.course_id = courses.id
GROUP BY department;

results.question: student_name list
results.baseline_sql: SELECT student_name FROM students;
results.system_sql:
SELECT name 
FROM students;

results.question: course score data
results.baseline_sql: SELECT * FROM course_score_data;
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, c.title, s.age, s.department, sc.marks, scd.exam_date
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id;

results.question: List students whose average marks are above the overall average
results.system_sql:
SELECT s.name, AVG(s.marks) AS avg_marks
FROM students s
JOIN scores sc ON s.id = sc.student_id
JOIN courses c ON sc.course_id = c.id
GROUP BY s.id
HAVING AVG(s.marks) > (SELECT AVG(sc2.marks) FROM scores sc2 JOIN students s2 ON sc2.student_id = s2.id);

results.question: Show course-wise toppers with student names
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT course_name, student_name
FROM students
JOIN enrollments ON students.student_id = enrollments.student_id
JOIN courses ON enrollments.course_id = courses.course_id
GROUP BY course_name, student_name
ORDER BY course_name;
results.system_sql:
SELECT c.title, s.name
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
GROUP BY c.id
ORDER BY s.marks DESC;

results.question: Count students scoring above 90 in each course
results.baseline_sql:
SELECT course, COUNT(*) as count_above_90
FROM grades
WHERE grade > 90
GROUP BY course;
results.system_sql:
SELECT c.code, c.title, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM courses c
JOIN scores s ON c.id = s.course_id
WHERE s.marks > 90
GROUP BY c.code, c.title;

Students who performed better than average in each course

Top department based on average marks
List students whose average marks are above the overall average
Show course-wise toppers with student names
Count students scoring above 90 in each course
